p0Workstreams.title: Define production API contract freeze process
p0Workstreams.rationale: Without a documented freeze process, the OpenAPI contract can change arbitrarily, making it impossible for integrators to rely on the API surface.
p0Workstreams.acceptanceGate: A documented contract freeze process and a versioned OpenAPI artifact with a non-RC version.

p0Workstreams.title: Confirm endpoint inventory and legacy path policy
p0Workstreams.rationale: Integrators need a complete, labeled inventory of every path so they can distinguish stable endpoints from experimental or deprecated ones.
p0Workstreams.acceptanceGate: An endpoint inventory document with maturity labels (stable / experimental / deprecated) for every path.

p0Workstreams.title: Standardize error response schema
p0Workstreams.rationale: Error responses are referenced consistently but may lack required fields, examples per status code, and strict schema enforcement across all endpoints.
p0Workstreams.acceptanceGate: OpenAPI ErrorResponse schema is complete and every error status code uses it consistently with examples.

p0Workstreams.title: Harden auth/permission contract documentation
p0Workstreams.rationale: Several endpoints document security blocks that are not fully enforced, creating a gap between the contract and implementation that must be documented.
p0Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Auth/permission contract document that aligns OpenAPI security blocks with current implementation truth and target state.

p0Workstreams.title: Validate OpenAPI path/schema coverage
p0Workstreams.rationale: Missing request examples, missing response examples, undocumented query parameters, and inconsistent field naming reduce integrator confidence.
p0Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Coverage report with zero undocumented public paths and zero schemas lacking examples.

p0Workstreams.title: Add CI gate for OpenAPI contract validation
p0Workstreams.rationale: Contract regressions must be caught before merge; structural and semantic validation of the OpenAPI document should be automated.
p0Workstreams.acceptanceGate: A CI stage that fails if OpenAPI is structurally invalid or if documented paths lack schemas.

p0Workstreams.title: Document compatibility and deprecation rules
p0Workstreams.rationale: Integrators need a guaranteed stability window and a clear communication channel for breaking changes.
p0Workstreams.acceptanceGate: A published compatibility and deprecation policy document with supported-version lifetimes.

p1Workstreams.title: Add richer request/response examples
p1Workstreams.rationale: Illustrative JSON examples for every request body and success response reduce onboarding time and surface contract ambiguities.
p1Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Every POST/PUT/PATCH request body and every 2xx response has at least one example or examples entry.

p1Workstreams.title: Add integrator-focused endpoint grouping
p1Workstreams.rationale: Grouping endpoints by Product Core area makes the OpenAPI document navigable for partners who think in workflow stages rather than paths.
p1Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Integrators can navigate the OpenAPI document by Product Core concept without reading the full path list.

p1Workstreams.title: Add planned-call smoke validation against documented paths
p1Workstreams.rationale: A machine-readable report mapping every OpenAPI path to at least one example or test fixture proves the contract is exercised.
p1Workstreams.acceptanceGate: A machine-readable report mapping every OpenAPI path to at least one example or test fixture.

p1Workstreams.title: Document pagination/filter/query conventions
p1Workstreams.rationale: List endpoints may accept pagination, filtering, and sorting in inconsistent ways; conventions must be standardized and documented.
p1Workstreams.acceptanceGate: A conventions document referenced by the OpenAPI spec with example query strings.

p1Workstreams.title: Align demo runner with API usage walkthrough
p1Workstreams.rationale: The GL-061 smoke manifest and the GL-058 walkthrough should reference the exact same OpenAPI paths in the same order.
p1Workstreams.acceptanceGate: A coherence test proving every walkthrough step has a matching smoke manifest entry.

p1Workstreams.title: Add contract review checklist for pilot partners
p1Workstreams.rationale: Partners need a short, checklist-driven way to evaluate whether the current contract meets their integration needs.
p1Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Partner-facing contract review checklist document with pass/fail criteria.

p2Workstreams.title: SDK/client generation readiness
p2Workstreams.rationale: Generated clients require a frozen contract and stable version; this is a post-pilot, post-freeze concern.
p2Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Generated Python and TypeScript clients compile and pass basic integration tests against a local server.

p2Workstreams.title: Versioned API policy
p2Workstreams.rationale: Formal URL or header versioning and a documented migration path between versions are required for long-term stability.
p2Workstreams.acceptanceGate: A versioned API policy document and a proof-of-concept versioned path.

p2Workstreams.title: Public changelog process
p2Workstreams.rationale: Integrators need visibility into contract changes; a machine-readable and human-readable changelog supports this.
p2Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Changelog exists and is updated automatically from merged contract changes.

p2Workstreams.title: Advanced schema linting
p2Workstreams.rationale: Adopting a linter enforces naming conventions, example coverage, and security scheme consistency at scale.
p2Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Linter passes in CI with a documented rule set.

p2Workstreams.title: External conformance test pack
p2Workstreams.rationale: Third-party conformance tests validate the running API against the OpenAPI contract, increasing integrator trust.
p2Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Conformance tests pass against a local GrantLayer instance.

p2Workstreams.title: Dashboard/API alignment after pilot validation
p2Workstreams.rationale: The HTML dashboard must use the same endpoints and field names as the documented contract to prevent drift.
p2Workstreams.acceptanceGate: Dashboard network traffic audit shows no usage of undocumented paths or fields.

notYetRecommended: Rename endpoints without a deprecation policy
Remove legacy paths without a communicated timeline
Add versioned public API surface before versioning policy is defined
Change auth behavior as part of contract review
Rewrite OpenAPI broadly without scoped issue

relatedArtifacts.description: Production readiness cut documenting blockers and permitted scope
OpenAPI contract validation test guarding expected and legacy paths
Step-by-step API-first walkthrough mapping Product Core stages to OpenAPI paths
Demo runner / API smoke script for local dry-run confidence
